text: Ever seen a foam explosion that looks like giant toothpaste? It’s all chemistry! This is called elephant toothpaste. It uses hydrogen peroxide, a catalyst, and dish soap to create a foamy eruption. When hydrogen peroxide decomposes, it releases oxygen. That’s what inflates the soap foam! Look at this tower! It’s a fun demonstration seen in science museums worldwide. But remember, safety first! Always have adult supervision when using concentrated peroxide. Want more amazing science? Follow for more experiments like this!
